name: gpt-oss-20b (High)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-experts transformer, 32 experts/layer, top-4 active, 24 layers
note: 21B MoE (3.6B active) open-weight sibling that runs on consumer hardware; Apache 2.0.
tpsNote: 225 tok/s at 8k context (Q4_K_M GGUF, llama.cpp) on RTX 4090; throughput collapses to ~9 tok/s at full 128k context.

results.notes: AIME 2024, no tools, high reasoning effort
GPQA Diamond, no tools, high reasoning effort
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools, high reasoning effort (Table 3 of the gpt-oss model card)
Codeforces Elo, no tools, high reasoning effort
Multilingual MMLU average, high reasoning effort
Tau-Bench Retail domain, high reasoning effort

TIGER-Lab's official MMLU-Pro leaderboard submissions dataset (raw results.csv fetched directly) has a row 'GPT-oss-20B(high)' with Overall=0.736 (73.6%), Data Source tagged 'Self-Reported' (i.e. OpenAI/a submitter provided the number rather than TIGER-Lab running it themselves -- contrast with the adjacent 'GPT-oss-20B(medium)' row at 0.7314, tagged 'TIGER-Lab' meaning independently run with full per-subject breakdown, which would apply to the separate gpt-oss-20b-medium.json file, not this High-effort file). MMLU-Pro is NOT one of the benchmarks in the original arXiv model card (2508.10925) table, so this is a genuinely separate evaluation from the already-tracked plain 'mmlu' score (85.3, high effort). No corresponding row exists for 'GPT-oss-20B(low)' on this leaderboard, so nothing new found there. evaluatedAt is the most recent commit timestamp on the results.csv file (2026-03-11), not necessarily the exact eval run date, since the CSV has no per-row date column.

verificationNotes: Same primary source as Medium config (arXiv 2508.10925 Table 3): gpt-oss-20b high = AIME2024(no tools) 92.1, GPQA Diamond(no tools) 71.5, MMLU 85.3, SWE-bench Verified 60.7 — all four match exactly. Params/arch/license/date verified identically. No changes made.
